title: pavonia urens
intro: Pavonia urens, also known as the sticky rose mallow, is a perennial herb belonging to the mallow family. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pavonia urens include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wound healing and to reduce fever. The herbal preparations of Pavonia urens include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be prepared by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or by mixing the plant's extracts with other natural ingredients.
